channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Passengers on the cruise ship struck by a Hantavirus outbreak flown to home countries
item.description: The passengers from the MV Hondius, the cruise ship at the center of the recent Hantavirus outbreak, are being evacuated - and flown home to their respective countries.
item.content:encoded: The passengers from the MV Hondius, the cruise ship at the center of the recent Hantavirus outbreak, are being evacuated - and flown home to their respective countries.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: Doomscrolling too much? Try these tips to put the phone down and plug into real life
item.description: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.content:encoded: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Arizona students show love for their teachers
item.description: As students across the country celebrate teachers and school staff, some thoughtful learners in Arizona articulate why they appreciate theirs.
item.content:encoded: As students across the country celebrate teachers and school staff, some thoughtful learners in Arizona articulate why they appreciate theirs.
item.dc:creator: Noor Haghighi

item.title: What's at stake for President Trump's upcoming visit to China
item.description: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to China under President Biden, Nicholas Burns, about what to expect from President Trump's upcoming visit to China.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to China under President Biden, Nicholas Burns, about what to expect from President Trump's upcoming visit to China.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Diving deep into the fascinating history of America's jaywalking laws
item.description: Jaywalking is often considered to be a pretty minor offense, but it is illegal in many American cities. KCUR's Mackenzie Martin offers a history of America's jaywalking laws.
item.content:encoded: Jaywalking is often considered to be a pretty minor offense, but it is illegal in many American cities. KCUR's Mackenzie Martin offers a history of America's jaywalking laws.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: Cineplexity: the best female action stars in the movies
item.description: NPR staff share their favorite female action stars, their surprising attributes and best scenes.
item.content:encoded: NPR staff share their favorite female action stars, their surprising attributes and best scenes.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: Wisconsin residents hold 'caravans of love' to celebrate mothers in prison
item.description: A group of Wisconsinites organizes a "headlight caravan" outside a local women's prison. It's a show of solidarity for the women inside the prison to let them know they're not forgotten.
item.content:encoded: A group of Wisconsinites organizes a "headlight caravan" outside a local women's prison. It's a show of solidarity for the women inside the prison to let them know they're not forgotten.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lehr

item.title: Why has China doubled its nuclear capacity in the last decade?
item.description: For decades, China pared back its nuclear weapons program and kept its arsenal to a minimum. Now, new satellite images show China is rapidly expanding its nuclear arsenal and production sites. Why?
item.content:encoded: For decades, China pared back its nuclear weapons program and kept its arsenal to a minimum. Now, new satellite images show China is rapidly expanding its nuclear arsenal and production sites. Why?
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Florida farmers struggle to adapt as disease and climate take toll on citrus industry
item.description: Florida's citrus industry is in deep decline and growers are trying to hang on as they find ways to withstand disease and disasters.
item.content:encoded: Florida's citrus industry is in deep decline and growers are trying to hang on as they find ways to withstand disease and disasters.
item.dc:creator: Tom Hudson

item.title: Tracking the hot-button issues driving this year's midterms politics
item.description: Record high oil prices, war with Iran, and controversial immigration policies are just some of the issues for voters. NPR's Domenico Montanaro talks about covering this year's complex midterms.
item.content:encoded: Record high oil prices, war with Iran, and controversial immigration policies are just some of the issues for voters. NPR's Domenico Montanaro talks about covering this year's complex midterms.
item.dc:creator: Elena Burnett
